conversations.content: Let's do an Azerothian legal knowledge Q&A to test your understanding of the legal provisions.

Please use the previously mentioned legal system of Azeroth to answer: How many gold coins should be fined for publicly defaming the goblin trade agreement in the Ratchet City Tavern without widely disseminating the content?
<function>memory_query_call</function><content>Fine amount for publicly defaming a goblin trade agreement in a Ratchet tavern under the previously mentioned Azerothian legal system</content>

Related memory fragment: [mem-id:619] Time: 2025-08-23-14:20, Content: The user taught the Azeroth Ratchet Trade Reputation Protection Law, the core of which is that those who publicly defame trade agreements will be fined 100 gold coins, required to post a letter of repentance at the tavern door for three days, and simultaneously prohibited from engaging in trading activities within the jurisdiction of the Chamber of Commerce for six months. If the defamatory content spreads widely, the fine will be increased to 500 gold coins and the offender will be expelled from Ratchet. Applicable to all taverns and Chamber of Commerce premises in Ratchet. Low relevance memory fragments: None.

conversations.think: There is 1 related entry mentioned in the memory. [mem-id:619] clearly states a fine of 100 gold coins, along with other penalties such as a letter of repentance and a trade ban. The user only asked about the fine amount, so I only need to answer this part.
conversations.content: According to the Azerothian world legal system, publicly defaming a goblin trade agreement in a Ratchet tavern results in a fine of 100 gold coins.
Correct! You have mastered this legal rule.
